Entertainment.headline: Cannes 2026 WINNERS: Cristian Mungiu's Fjord wins Palme d'Or, Minotaur takes Grand Prix
Entertainment.detail: Director Cristian Mungiu won the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival for the film 'Fjord,' while 'Minotaur' took the Grand Prix. The awards highlight the culmination of the festival, celebrating outstanding achievements in cinema.

Entertainment.headline: Osbourne Biopic: Son Jack Has 'Big, Brilliant Actor' In Mind To Portray Ozzy
Entertainment.detail: Jack Osbourne confirmed that the biopic about his father, Ozzy Osbourne, is moving forward at Sony Pictures and mentioned they have a 'big, brilliant actor' in mind to play the Black Sabbath frontman. The film, in development since 2021, will explore Ozzy's life and career alongside his wife and manager, Sharon Osbourne.

Entertainment.headline: Culture minister congratulates film director Jin Mi-song on Cannes win
Entertainment.detail: Culture Minister Chae Hwi-young congratulated Korean film director Jin Mi-song for winning Second Prize in the La Cinef section at the Cannes Film Festival for “Silent Voices”. The film follows a day in the life of a Korean immigrant family in New York and was selected from 2,747 global submissions.

Cambodian authorities raided two 'adult entertainment' bars suspected of illegal activities and exploitation, rescuing 35 women and minors. One suspect, described as a manager or supervisor, was detained for legal action as part of an ongoing nationwide campaign.
Entertainment.headline: Cambodian authorities raid two riverside 'adult entertainment' bars, rescue 35 victims, detain one suspect
Stephen Colbert Makes Surprise Return to TV 1 Day After Final CBS Show
Entertainment.detail: Stephen Colbert made a surprise return to television one day after his final CBS show, marking the end of 'The Late Show' after its 33-year run. Colbert's farewell was a warm, star-studded goodbye.

Sports.headline: Knicks move within 1 win of NBA Finals with 121-108 victory over Cavaliers
Sports.detail: The New York Knicks defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 121-108, putting them one win away from reaching the NBA Finals. The Knicks are now leading the series 3-0.

Sports.headline: French Open starts amid a heat wave in Paris
Sports.detail: The French Open has commenced in Paris, with Novak Djokovic scheduled to play against Perricard in the night match amidst a heat wave. Alex Eala is also garnering attention ahead of her French Open campaign.
Nikolaj Ehlers scored in overtime to lead the Hurricanes to a victory over the Canadiens in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Final. With the Hurricanes' win, the series is now tied 1-1.
Sports.headline: Ehlers' OT goal lifts Hurricanes past Canadiens in Game 2 of Eastern Conference Final
Ross Chastain wins the Charbroil 300 at Charlotte Motor Speedway
Sports.detail: Ross Chastain emerged victorious at the Charbroil 300 held at Charlotte Motor Speedway. Highlights and reactions from Ross Chastain, Jesse Love, and Austin Hill after the race are available.

Sports.headline: Dodgers bullpen producing record streak of scoreless innings even without injured closer Edwin Diaz
Sports.detail: The Los Angeles Dodgers' bullpen is on a record-breaking scoreless innings streak, even with Edwin Diaz sidelined due to injury. Teoscar Hernández had a standout performance with 6 RBIs in the Dodgers' 11-3 win over the Brewers.

Science.detail: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body's ability to absorb healthy flavanols, showing that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.
Science.headline: Surprising Research Reveals Why You Shouldn't Add Bananas to Your Smoothies

Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered the oldest known hand-held wooden tools ever used by humans — and they're an astonishing 430,000 years old.
Science.headline: Scientists Discover the Oldest Wooden Tools Ever Used by Humans

Science.detail: An AI-powered system scanned 400,000 Reddit posts and found previously unknown side effects of Ozempic. The findings suggest AI could turn social media into a valuable resource for identifying drug side effects.
Science.headline: AI Scans 400,000 Reddit Posts and Finds Hidden Ozempic Side Effects

Science.detail: Scientists have directly watched angular momentum move through a crystal for the very first time and discovered a bizarre twist along the way using ultra-powerful terahertz laser pulses, triggering tiny atomic rotations inside a crystal.
Science.headline: Scientists Discover Atoms Suddenly Spinning Backward in Quantum Experiment
Scientists Warn Popular Vitamin D Supplement May Have a “Previously Unknown” Downside
Science.detail: A surprising study suggests vitamin D2 supplements may reduce the body's levels of vitamin D3 — the more effective form of vitamin D.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The U.K.'s grocery store price controls are political desperation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The flailing Labour government leans on grocery stores to cap prices, a sign of political desperation as Prime Minister Keir Starmer's unpopularity grows and nearly a quarter of Labour members of parliament have called for his resignation. The government has reportedly threatened additional regulatory interventions if retailers refuse to cap prices.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Seniors don't need the federal government to buy them bath mats
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The opinion argues against Sen. Angus King's proposal for the government to send every Medicare recipient a bath mat to reduce falls, stating that seniors are adults who should take responsibility for themselves. The editorial questions where the government's mandate to protect seniors ends and suggests that seniors should be able to afford basic household goods themselves.

Eli Lilly's latest GLP-1 drug, retatrutide, shows promising results in clinical trials, helping people shed more than 30 percent of their body weight, which is on par with gastric bypass surgery. If the results hold up, the medication could transform care for severe obesity; however, the results are yet to be peer-reviewed, and the drug comes with side effects.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: The promise of weight-loss drugs keeps growing
EDITORIAL: Human rights compliance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Taipei Times discusses Taiwan's progress on human rights compliance, noting that while the government has constructively engaged with reviews, a significant number of key recommendations from previous reviews remain unimplemented or have only been partially addressed. Concerns include the lack of progress on the rights of asylum seekers and refugees, the death penalty, and the operation of the National Human Rights Commission.

Mario Nicolais writes that Governor Polis of Colorado has drawn criticism for commuting the sentence of election denier Tina Peters, despite pleas from Democratic legislators, a Republican prosecutor, and election officials. The action is seen as emboldening conspiracy theorists and endangering election workers, who have already been targeted with threats and violence.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Nicolais: Polis put election workers and democracy in the crosshairs by commuting Tina Peters' sentence

Politics.headline: Clashes in Belgrade as student-led protests challenge Vucic
Politics.detail: Demonstrators are challenging President Vucic's rule and demanding elections, justice, and the rule of law in Belgrade.
A suspect was killed after exchanging fire with the Secret Service near the White House.
Politics.headline: Suspect shot dead after opening fire near White House checkpoint, Secret Service says
As war evolves, more female troops make the ultimate sacrifice
Politics.detail: On Memorial Day, military families remember the Iran conflict's fallen and navigate heartbreak without mothers and wives.
A recent report indicates that the UK's supply chain is not adequately prepared for major disruptions such as war.
Politics.headline: UK supply chain unprepared for major shocks such as war, report warns
A Democratic populist tries a comeback in a red state
Politics.detail: Former senator Sherrod Brown speaks during a campaign event in Austintown, Ohio, last month.

Health.headline: Newly Identified Brain Protein May Play a Major Role in How the Body Ages
Health.detail: Researchers discovered that declining levels of Menin in the hypothalamus triggered inflammation, memory problems, and bone issues, suggesting this protein's crucial role in the aging process.
A surprising study reveals that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually absorbs, as bananas interfere with flavanol absorption from berries.
Health.headline: Adding Bananas to Berry Smoothies Can Dramatically Reduce the Body's Ability to Absorb Healthy Flavanols

Health.detail: A genetic analysis of nearly 500 cat tumors from around the world has provided new insights into feline cancer, potentially leading to improved diagnostics and treatments.
Health.headline: Scientists Crack Open the “Black Box” of Feline Cancer in Landmark Study

Health.detail: New research explores the specific brain mechanisms that translate our intentions into physical actions, potentially offering insights into movement disorders and rehabilitation strategies.
Health.headline: How a distinct communication subspace in the brain turns goals into actions

Health.detail: The surprising discovery shows that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually absorbs.
Health.headline: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body's ability to absorb healthy flavanols.

Technology.detail: ERP software provider Epicor announces new AI offerings and services at its annual conference, promising 90-day cloud ERP deployments.
Technology.headline: Epicor Debuts Agentic AI Platform And Promises 90-Day Cloud ERP Deployments
Google Overhauls Top-Tier AI Plans, Drops Price and Adds YouTube Premium
Technology.detail: Google is revamping its top-tier AI plans, reducing the price, and including YouTube Premium in the package. This is the largest overhaul of Google search in almost three decades.

Technology.headline: Sadiq Khan sparks row with Met after blocking £50m AI deal with Palantir
Technology.detail: Mayor of London Sadiq Khan is in a dispute with the Metropolitan Police after blocking a £50 million AI contract with Palantir. Palantir has responded to Sadiq Khan after the contract was blocked.

Technology.headline: Minister urges mindset shift in tech push
Technology.detail: Malaysia's Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang stated that the biggest challenges in advancing science and technology are mindset-related, such as ignorance, lack of confidence and fear of new technologies. He urged students to embrace and master AI rather than fear it.

Following its 2026 Investor Day, Amkor Technology (AMKR) has focused on its long-term financial goals and a multi-phase Arizona expansion for advanced semiconductor packaging. The most followed narrative pegs Amkor Technology's fair value at $60, which sits below the last close of $65.75 and frames the current debate around upside versus execution risk.
Technology.headline: A Look At Amkor Technology (AMKR) Valuation After 2026 Investor Day And Arizona Packaging Expansion Plans

Education.headline: Japan may add farming and anime courses to technical colleges
Education.detail: The education ministry is considering adding new courses to kōsen technical colleges beyond those in industrial fields, such as agriculture, as well as manga and animation, to develop talent in a wide range of areas.

Education.headline: Thousands flock to Star Education Fair 2026
Education.detail: Thousands of school students and their families are attending the Star Education Fair 2026 to explore tertiary pathways, meet education institutions, and learn about scholarship opportunities.

Education.headline: With AI now reading student names at graduation, not everyone is applauding
Education.detail: Schools are experimenting with AI to pronounce graduate names at commencement ceremonies, aiming for accuracy and efficiency, but the technology has faced some pushback and malfunctions.

Education.headline: 'STEM education must evolve'
Education.detail: Experts emphasize that STEM education must evolve to include AI literacy and encourage students to integrate emerging technologies into their fields, highlighting the development of AI tools like a 'study buddy' to aid students across subjects.
A letter to the editor argues that AI-generated lesson plans undermine teachers and shortchange students.
Education.headline: Artificial intelligence is ruining education

Zimbabwe.detail: Exiled MDC founding member Elliot Pfebve has died in the UK after a battle with cancer.
Zimbabwe.headline: Exiled MDC founding member Elliot Pfebve dies after cancer battle in the UK
Simba Bhora coach Mandla Mpofu takes full blame after 4-1 defeat to Herentals
Zimbabwe.detail: Simba Bhora coach Mandla Mpofu accepts responsibility following a 4-1 loss to Herentals.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa ally Temba Mliswa blasts arrest of Sonja and Tabitha Madzikanda
Zimbabwe.detail: Temba Mliswa, a Mnangagwa ally, has criticized the arrest of Sonja and Tabitha Madzikanda, arguing that the dispute linked to businessman Wicknell Chivayo should have remained a civil matter and also launched a scathing attack on Zimbabwe Republic Police Commissioner General Stephen Mutamba, accusing the police force of corruption and abuse of power.

Zimbabwe.headline: SADC PF committees end session with push for action on governance, rights and inclusion
Zimbabwe.detail: SADC PF committees concluded their session by advocating for action on governance, rights, and inclusion.

Zimbabwe.headline: Ailing NRZ to get US$150milllion for infrastructure revamp but MPs fret over failure to meet targets
Zimbabwe.detail: The struggling NRZ is set to receive US$150 million for infrastructure upgrades, but Members of Parliament are worried about the company's history of not achieving its goals.

World.headline: 2 dead, dozens wounded after Russia launches large attack on Ukraine
World.detail: Russia used a powerful hypersonic missile with nuclear capabilities to carry out a massive attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, wounding dozens.

World.headline: 24 killed, dozens more injured in suicide bombing in Pakistan
World.detail: A suicide car bomb attack on a train in Pakistan resulted in at least 24 fatalities and numerous injuries, with the Prime Minister condemning it as a cowardly act of terrorism.

World.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
World.detail: An explosion in a mine in northern Shanxi province, China, has resulted in at least 82 deaths, with authorities urged to spare no effort in rescue operations.

World.headline: Gunman shot dead by Secret Service agents near White House: What we know
World.detail: A gunman was fatally shot by Secret Service agents near the White House; details surrounding the incident are still emerging.
The US and Iran are reportedly moving closer to a deal aimed at ending the war between them.
World.headline: US, Iran inch closer to deal to end the war: What to know

Finance & Economy.detail: Argentina is nearing the settlement of its debt with China's central bank, unwinding a currency lifeline amid geopolitical tensions between Washington and Beijing; the repayment is expected to be completed by mid-2026. Central bank records indicate that nearly 90% of the funds have already been repaid.
Finance & Economy.headline: Milei guts China currency lifeline after Trump ultimatum on financial ties: reports

Finance & Economy.detail: A new study shows startup activity has rebounded since the pandemic, with millions of Americans launching businesses in 2025; however, more people are starting businesses out of financial necessity, raising concerns about job stability. The rise in entrepreneurship may reflect a broader shift in how Americans build careers and piece together income.
Finance & Economy.headline: New Kauffman study finds entrepreneurship is bouncing back — but that's not all good news
At least £325bn of 'dirty money' flows through UK each year, says report
Finance & Economy.detail: A new report estimates that at least £325bn of illicit funds, linked to financial crime, money laundering, and tax evasion, flow through the UK annually, which equates to over 10% of the UK's GDP. The Finance Innovation Lab is urging Labour ministers to address the UK's role in enabling economic crime and tax evasion.

Surging gas prices disproportionately affect lower-income households, with commuting fuel costs consuming about 4% of their income compared to less than 1% for higher-income households. The gap has widened since March due to the U.S. war on Iran and stalled oil shipments.
Finance & Economy.headline: Trump said gas prices are 'peanuts.' Only if you're rich.

Finance & Economy.detail: The British Treasury has been pressuring grocery chains to adopt voluntary price caps on staples due to price increases worsened by the conflict in Iran. The government has threatened regulatory interventions if retailers refuse to cap prices, despite acknowledging potential financial damage to grocers.
Finance & Economy.headline: The U.K.'s grocery store price controls are political desperation

Lifestyle.headline: Why Randeep Hooda Is 'In Teenage Love' After Welcoming Baby With Lin Laishram
Lifestyle.detail: Randeep Hooda discusses his joy after welcoming a baby with Lin Laishram.

Lifestyle.headline: The Art Of Doing Less: A Slow Weekend In The Hills Of Uttarakhand
Lifestyle.detail: An article describing a relaxing weekend getaway in the hills of Uttarakhand.
Jwala Gutta shares her experience of donating 60 litres of breast milk in her first postpartum year.
Lifestyle.headline: Jwala Gutta On Donating 60 Litres Of Breast Milk In First Postpartum Year

Lifestyle.detail: Details on Thailand's visa program that allows Indians to stay for up to 5 years for approximately Rs 25,000.
Lifestyle.headline: Thailand's Rs 25,000 Visa Lets Indians Stay For Up To 5 Years. How It Works
Inside The 'Twin Town' In Kerala Where 1 In Every 5 People Is A Twin
Lifestyle.detail: A look inside a town in Kerala where a significant portion of the population consists of twins.

Crime & Justice.detail: In Honduras, at least 25 people were killed in two separate attacks by gunmen.
Crime & Justice.headline: Gunmen open fire in 2 separate attacks in Honduras, killing at least 25 people
Former F1 champion Alain Prost reportedly injured as police investigate robbery at family home
Crime & Justice.detail: Police are investigating a robbery at the family home of former F1 champion Alain Prost, where he was reportedly injured.
Britney Spears was described as 'confrontational' and 'flamboyant' but had low alcohol levels during a DUI stop.
Crime & Justice.headline: Britney Spears was 'confrontational' and 'flamboyant' but tested low for alcohol during DUI stop
LA parents who decapitated their kids sentenced for 'monstrous crimes'
Crime & Justice.detail: California parents, Natalie Sumiko Brothwell and Maurice Jewel Taylor Sr., were sentenced for the 'monstrous crimes' of decapitating their children.

Crime & Justice.headline: 1 teen dead, 1 critically hurt after subway surfing on Williamsburg Bridge
Crime & Justice.detail: One teenager is dead and another critically injured after subway surfing on the Williamsburg Bridge in New York City.
